results.explanation: The headline inference: 4 asserted edges entail the never-asserted 1→5 pair via transitive precedesStep.

results.displayName: Inferred delegation Release Manager to CTO
results.explanation: Escalation chain RM→VP Eng→CTO: the RM→CTO hop is never asserted; transitive delegatesTo entails it.
The multi-substrate equivalence claim: reasoner and Postgres views disagree on zero computed values.
Every row is a globally-addressable individual; the workflow's IRI is derived, stable, and identical on both engines.
Gate IRI derives through the step's path chain (lookup → calc chain).
Role→agent indirection: the step's executing agent type is derived through AssignedRole→FilledBy*.

results.displayName: Gate step executing agent type resolves
results.explanation: The approval-gate step resolves to its human Release Manager through the same one-edge chain.
COUNTIFS over the DERIVED IsExecutedByAI child field — conditional aggregation over a computed column.
The human side of the agent mix.
Boolean derived from the AI step count — an aggregation feeding a calc.

results.displayName: Gate step is human-filled at seed
results.explanation: requiresHumanApproval is satisfied at seed: the gate role is filled by a human.
The consistency rule does NOT fire on the seed facts — the clean baseline the mutation test breaks.

results.displayName: Workflow has no violation at seed
results.explanation: Workflow-level rollup of approval violations is false on seed facts.

results.displayName: Workflow status is a SKOS concept
results.explanation: workflowStatus points into the WorkflowStatusConcepts scheme.

results.displayName: Role capability is a SKOS concept
results.explanation: hasCapability points into the AgentCapabilityConcepts scheme.
Heuristic 6: label/comment documentation travels with the model into every substrate.
The gate's defining datatype property (raw, gate-only).
INDEX/MATCH lookup gate→step→role. KNOWN GAP CANDIDATE: both engines currently drop lookup columns from their individuals payload — this test exists to keep that gap visible until fixed.

results.displayName: Gate approver human resolves via lookup
results.explanation: Second-order lookup gate→role→human filler. Same known gap candidate as GateRole.

results.displayName: Release Manager has exactly one filler
results.explanation: Functional filledBy: exactly one of the three filler arms is set.

results.displayName: No roles with bad filler cardinality
results.explanation: Suite-4 cardinality witness: zero roles violate the exactly-one-filler rule on seed facts.

results.displayName: Release authorization derives from legal clearance
results.explanation: One link of the 5-artifact PROV wasDerivedFrom chain.

results.displayName: Risk report attributed to the risk AI
results.explanation: PROV attribution: the artifact names the AI agent that produced it.

results.displayName: Post-deploy report has a derivation parent
results.explanation: Boolean derived from the lineage link.
consumesDataset points at the dcat:Dataset; answers CQ8.
ownedBy chain step→role→department resolves to Legal.
Same chain resolving to Engineering.
The cross-department join CQ7 asks about.
The resolved SequencePosition (override-if-pinned, else PrecedingStepCount+1) computes identically in both substrates; step 4 has four transitive predecessors and no override, so it resolves to 4.
Drives the time-budget bar; raw datatype property.
4 asserted edges.
6 pairs exist only by inference (10-pair closure minus 4 asserted).
The 10-pair transitive closure, counted from the materialized closure rows.
Time-dependent calc (months granularity) — the staleness clock.
The CQ5 governance query on the seed facts.
The higher-compliance-risk join on the seed facts.
Steward vs Authority: only the Authority approves.
The other half of the governance split.
Semantic-versioning derivation from ChangeKind.
The minor release is back-compat by derivation.
Bitemporal validity: the open-ended assignment is current.

results.displayName: Assignment active as of audit date
results.explanation: The 'who filled this on the audit date' time-travel query.
AI→Human turnover detection across consecutive assignments.
The audit trigger derived from the agent-type change.
Workflow-level count of audit-triggering changes.
AI-system-registry blast radius: artifacts this AI produced.
Blast radius: workflows reachable from this AI's artifacts.
The audit anchor: which model version produced the attributed artifacts.
When the audited model version went live.

results.displayName: Assign AI to the human-only gate fires the violation
results.explanation: Suite-4 inject-an-error: filling the gate role with an AI must flip ApprovalConsistencyViolation on the step AND roll up to the workflow, on BOTH engines.

results.displayName: Backdating the review makes the workflow stale
results.explanation: Backdate dct:modified past the staleness window: IsStale must flip true.

results.displayName: Backdating fires the stale-AND-AI compliance join
results.explanation: Seed facts already include AI-executed steps, so staleness alone completes the IsStaleAndHasAIAgent join.

results.displayName: Dropping the sign-off requirement clears the violation
results.explanation: The violation is an AND of requiresHumanApproval and a non-human filler: with the requirement off, an AI filler is consistent.
